name: Gmsh (3D Finite Element Mesh Generator)
pricing_notes: Open source (GPL-2.0+); Christophe Geuzaine / Jean-François Remacle

performance.notes: C++ backend via Python API; complex meshing of large geometries can be slow

agent_readiness.notes: 3D finite element mesh generator with OpenCASCADE and built-in CAD kernel. import gmsh. gmsh.initialize(). gmsh.model.add('box'). gmsh.model.occ.addBox(0,0,0, 1,1,1). gmsh.model.occ.synchronize(). gmsh.model.mesh.generate(3). gmsh.write('mesh.msh'). gmsh.finalize(). 1D/2D/3D mesh generation. Mesh algorithms: Frontal-Delaunay, Packing-Parallelograms. Mesh format: MSH4, VTK, CGNS, STL, Gmsh native. Physical groups for BCs. STEP/IGES import via OCC. OpenCASCADE geometry: gmsh.model.occ.*. Transfinite structured meshes. Refinement fields. gmsh.fltk.run() for GUI.

gotchas: Must call occ.synchronize() after geometry operations before meshing
GPL-2.0+ license — copyleft for commercial distributions
No type annotations — poor IDE support
Error messages are often cryptic; geometry repair needed for complex STEP imports
API is low-level; verbose for simple tasks
best_when: FEA preprocessing: generating hex/tet meshes for finite element solvers
Meshing complex 3D geometries imported from STEP/IGES
Structured Transfinite meshes for CFD boundary layers
When FEniCS, OpenFOAM, or Elmer FEM needs mesh input
avoid_when: Simple parametric geometry (use CadQuery or FreeCAD)
High-quality surface remeshing for graphics (use Blender/Meshmixer)
When CGAL or Tetgen is sufficient for simpler needs
summary: Gmsh generates finite element meshes for FEA/CFD. gmsh.initialize(); addBox(); occ.synchronize(); mesh.generate(3); write('mesh.msh'). OpenCASCADE geometry kernel. STEP/IGES import. MSH4, VTK, CGNS output. Physical groups for boundary conditions. Transfinite structured meshes. Refinement fields. GPL-2.0+ by Geuzaine/Remacle. Standard for FEA preprocessing. Must synchronize() after geometry ops. Cryptic errors. Verbose API.
